name: Dahan River Bikeway (Yingge section)
notes: Flat riverside trail, well-shaded, easy parking at Yingge end

name: Jiaoban Mountain Trail (Sanxia)
notes: Short loop ~2.5km, good views of Sanxia valley; use trekking poles

name: Bitan Scenic Area (Xindian)
notes: Suspension bridge, shaded banks, gentle terrain; riverside fishing popular near the bridge

name: Banqiao 435 Art Zone Garden
notes: Sculpture garden, flat, benches frequent; accessible toilets

name: Tucheng Chenfu Road Riverside Park
notes: 10-min drive from Shulin, very flat, often breezy

name: Longmen Camping Meadow (Sanxia)
notes: Open meadow by Sanxia River, beautiful on clear days; uneven ground

name: Shulin Riverside Greenway
notes: Local favourite ~5km flat loop, shaded sections, water stations

name: Yingge Ceramics Museum Park
notes: Outdoor sculpture garden, paved paths, interesting for all ages

name: Sanxia Old Street & Zushi Temple
notes: Best in morning before crowds; cobblestones can be tricky — bring a cane

name: Zhonghe Yuantong Mountain Trail
notes: Shaded climb ~3km, cooler than valley floor, good workout

name: Banqiao Lin Family Garden
notes: Historic garden, very flat, shaded by large banyan trees

name: Tamsui Old Street & Waterfront
notes: Worth the 50km drive — waterfront promenade, sunset views

name: Banqiao Station Underground Mall Walk
notes: Covered, climate-controlled, no wind; good fallback on breezy days

name: Sanxia Dasi Bikeway (lower section)
notes: Flat, riverside section sheltered by tree line; wind-protected

name: Tucheng Sports Center Indoor Track
notes: Fully covered 200m indoor track, climate-controlled, very accessible

name: Xinzhuang Lihui Park
notes: Large sheltered pavilions, community tai chi groups in morning

name: Shulin Community Center Courtyard
notes: Covered walkway, easy access, good for overcast days

name: Banqiao Far Eastern Department Store Sky Garden
notes: Rooftop garden accessible by elevator; protected terrace on drizzly days

name: Fuzhong Library Garden (Banciao)
notes: Quiet library garden courtyard, sheltered veranda, good AQI days only

name: Sanxia Visitor Center & Museum
notes: Fully indoor, free entry, interesting local history exhibits

name: Wuliaojian Mountain Trail (Shulin)
notes: Rugged 5km loop with ridgeline views; steep sections, bring poles and water

name: Mianhua Mountain Trail (Sanxia)
notes: Gentle forested climb, popular with early morning tai chi walkers

name: Pingxi Shifen Waterfall Trail
notes: Impressive waterfall 60km from Shulin; best on sunny clear days, slippery when wet

name: Jiufen Old Street & Teahouses
notes: Scenic hillside village 65km away; misty on overcast days, stunning on clear ones

name: Tamsui Fisherman's Wharf Promenade
notes: Suspension bridge, sunset views over river mouth; breezy — bring a light jacket

name: Guandu Nature Park (Beitou)
notes: Wetland habitat, excellent for birdwatching at low tide; flat, accessible paths

name: Linkou Kite Flying Park (Linkou Plateau)
notes: Open plateau with reliable wind for kite flying; exposed, bring sun protection

name: Shisizhang Waterfall Trail (Sanxia)
notes: Cascading 14-tier waterfall trail; slippery after rain, excellent on dry clear days

name: Daan Forest Park (Taipei)
notes: Large urban park with lake, cycling paths, and shaded walking trails; accessible by MRT

name: Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall Park
notes: Grand plaza and formal gardens; very exposed at midday in summer, pleasant in mild weather

name: Maokong Gondola + Tea Trail (Wenshan)
notes: Aerial gondola with mountain views; tea plantation walks above the city; avoid on foggy days

name: Bihu Park (Neihu)
notes: Lakeside park with cycling loop, shaded paths, and egret-watching spots

name: Xinhai Wetlands Nature Park (Wenshan)
notes: Urban wetland reserve, excellent bird species on clear mornings; bring binoculars

name: Bali Left Bank Rivermouth Park
notes: Flat riverside cycling path opposite Tamsui; scenic river views, free bike rental nearby

name: Tianmu Dadao Riverside Trail (Shilin)
notes: Tree-lined riverside walk through Tianmu; popular with expat community, well-maintained

name: Wulai Waterfall & Hot Spring (Wulai)
notes: Riverside hot spring village 40km south; waterfall viewpoint accessible by cable car

name: Sanxia Chengfu Riverside Park
notes: Quiet riverside path beside Sanxia River, less crowded than main bikeway; good fishing spots along the bank

name: Tucheng Sacred Heart Campus Forest Walk
notes: Wooded campus perimeter with mature trees, good shade, peaceful on weekdays

name: Shulin Daguan Road Canal Greenway
notes: Tree-shaded canal-side path through residential Shulin, very local and quiet

name: Neihu Technology Park Riverside Walk
notes: Flat riverside greenway beside the Jilong River, good for a brisk midday walk

name: Sanchong Zhongyang Riverside Park
notes: Wide riverside park on Danshui River, open and flat; some wind shelter near tree line

name: Chunhe Arts Village (Shulin)
notes: Small repurposed industrial arts space with open courtyard; interesting weekend markets

name: Banqiao Fuzhong Greenway
notes: Tree-lined urban greenway connecting MRT Fuzhong to Xinpu; sheltered by mature trees

name: Zhonghe Jingmei Riverside Greenway
notes: Smooth riverside bike path, flat, connects to Xindian bikeway network

name: Xinzhuang Guangming Road Park Strip
notes: Linear park along Guangming Road with exercise stations and benches

name: Luzhou Zhengyi Road Canal Walk
notes: Quiet canal-side promenade with shade trees; popular with local elderly for morning walks

name: Zhuwei Fisherman's Wharf (Tamsui)
notes: Small fishing harbour with fresh seafood stalls; more local feel than the main Tamsui waterfront

name: Jinzhong Mountain Trail (Xindian)
notes: Light hike through pine forest with Xindian reservoir views; ~3km, suitable for all fitness levels

name: Tucheng Zhengde Trail
notes: Low-elevation trail through Tucheng hills, shaded, moderate gradient, about 4km return

name: Gongliao Fulong Beach & Bikeway
notes: Northeast coast 90km away — pristine beach and riverside cycling; best on clear sunny days

name: Yingge Old Street Covered Arcade
notes: Covered arcade strip with ceramics galleries; naturally sheltered from drizzle

name: Shulin Train Station Square (Sheltered Canopy Walk)
notes: Covered pedestrian area around station; all-weather walkable, connects to nearby shops

name: Zhonghe Cultural Center Indoor Garden
notes: Cultural center with sheltered courtyard garden; indoor galleries open daily

name: Banqiao Jinhua Market Covered Arcade
notes: Traditional covered market arcade, busy mornings; keep dry while browsing local produce

name: Chunhe Library Courtyard (Shulin)
notes: Quiet library courtyard with benches; semi-sheltered, peaceful for a slow morning

name: Banqiao MRT Station Concourse Walk
notes: Extended underground concourse linking Banqiao HSR and metro stations; fully weatherproof

name: Xinzhuang Sports Center Indoor Court
notes: Multi-purpose indoor sports hall with badminton courts; warm and dry on rainy days

name: Luzhou Gongzheng Park (Covered Pavilions)
notes: Park with large covered pavilions used by elderly for morning exercises; wind-sheltered

name: Zhonghe Nanshan Park (Covered Pavilions)
notes: Hillside park with covered rest pavilions; good wind shelter, popular with retirees

name: Tucheng Huacheng Road Covered Commercial Strip
notes: Low-rise covered shopping strip; all-weather walkable, cafes and noodle shops

name: Wugu Industrial Park Walking Path (Low Wind Corridor)
notes: Sheltered walking path along industrial canal; buildings block prevailing wind effectively

name: Yonghe Xiyuan Road Covered Market Walk
notes: Traditional covered market; good for a slow browse and breakfast on drizzly mornings

name: Banqiao New City Hall Arcade
notes: Modern civic complex with covered ground floor arcades; free entry, accessible

name: New Taipei City Museum of Art (Banciao)
notes: Contemporary art museum, air-conditioned, interesting rotating exhibitions

name: Yingge Ceramics Museum (Indoor Galleries)
notes: World-class ceramics collection, interactive exhibits, fully air-conditioned; café on-site

name: New Taipei City Hakka Museum (Sanxia)
notes: Excellent Hakka cultural exhibits, bilingual displays, free entry; near Zushi Temple

name: Shulin Xiulin District Library
notes: Clean, quiet public library; air-conditioned, good newspapers, perfect for rainy days

name: Tucheng Sports Center Indoor Pool
notes: 50m indoor lap pool, member access required; cool escape on sweltering days too

name: Banciao Cultural Center Exhibition Hall
notes: Multi-hall exhibition centre with rotating local and national art shows; free admission

name: Banqiao Mega City Mall (Indoor Walks)
notes: Large air-conditioned mall, good for extended rainy-day walks between food courts

name: Xindian Big City Mall
notes: Riverside mall near Xindian MRT station; good food court, full indoor circuit

name: Miramar Entertainment Park (Neihu)
notes: Indoor mall with a giant Ferris wheel; the wheel operates rain or shine (glass cabin)

name: Xinzhuang Cultural Arts Center
notes: Arts centre with semi-outdoor sculpture court and indoor gallery; pleasant even on overcast days

name: Taipei Main Station Underground Mall
notes: Massive underground pedestrian network; stay dry while exploring dozens of shops and restaurants

name: Gongguan Commercial District (Cafes & Bookshops)
notes: Student-heavy area near NTU with covered arcades and cosy independent cafes; good rainy-day destination

name: Shulin Sanhe Temple Covered Walkway
notes: Covered colonnade around local temple; sheltered from wind and light rain, peaceful morning stroll

name: Dahan River Bikeway (Tucheng section)
notes: Quiet upstream section of the Dahan bikeway; fewer cyclists, good bird-watching and fishing spots along the bank

name: Dahan River Bikeway (Banqiao section)
notes: Smooth paved riverside path under Banqiao bridges; very popular weekend cycling route

name: Xinpu Metro Park (Banciao)
notes: Spacious urban park near Xinpu MRT; large pavilion provides wind and light rain shelter

name: Luzhou Xincheng Park (Sheltered Grove)
notes: Dense tree canopy provides natural shelter; elderly tai chi groups gather here daily

name: Heping Island Coastal Park (Keelung)
notes: Dramatic volcanic rock formations and coastal pools 75km away; best on clear, calm days

name: Bali Shihsanhang Museum of Archaeology
notes: Award-winning archaeology museum in Bali; air-conditioned, excellent exhibits on prehistoric Taiwan

name: Xizhi Kangning Riverside Park
notes: Flat riverside cycling path along Jilong River, shaded by willows; connects to greater river network

name: New Taipei City Government Plaza (Banciao)
notes: Open civic plaza with fountain feature and walking paths; partially sheltered by arcade overhangs

name: Sanxia Triangle Park
notes: Small public park adjacent to Zushi Temple; good benches, shaded, central location in Sanxia

name: Tamsui Fort San Domingo & Red Castle
notes: Historic Dutch-era fort with gardens and estuary views; mostly paved, moderate incline

name: Yangmingshan National Park (Qingtiangang Grassland)
notes: High-altitude volcanic grassland with panoramic views; 60km from Shulin, best on clear days only

name: Banqiao Xibei Park
notes: Neighbourhood park with exercise stations and covered pavilion; good for an easy morning stretch

name: Shulin Indoor Public Swimming Pool
notes: Community indoor pool; comfortable year-round regardless of weather outside

name: Wugu Sports Park Indoor Gymnasium
notes: Multi-sport indoor gymnasium with basketball and badminton courts; warm and dry

name: Danhai Light Rail Promenade (Tamsui)
notes: New coastal district light rail promenade; ocean views, mostly flat, some wind exposure

name: Sijhih Bihu Trailhead (Xizhi)
notes: Mountain trail with lake views, ~4km moderate loop; refreshingly cool at altitude

name: Xindian Tonghou Riverside Greenway
notes: Scenic riverside greenway along Xindian Creek, shaded by bamboo and willows; connects to Bitan

name: Sanxia Yingdi Park
notes: Neighbourhood park with large covered pavilion and exercise equipment; local gathering spot

name: Tucheng Chengcang Park (Covered Pavilion)
notes: Terraced hillside park with large roofed pavilion; wind-sheltered, good for gentle morning exercise

name: Shulin Fuxing Park
notes: Quiet neighbourhood park with mature shade trees and covered rest area; 5 minutes from Shulin Station

name: Banqiao Xingmin Sports Park
notes: 200m rubberised jogging track with outdoor fitness stations; open 24 hours, well-lit at night

name: Sanchong Indoor Sports Complex
notes: Indoor multi-purpose sports facility with 200m track; climate-controlled and fully weather-proof

name: Xinzhuang Night Market (Daytime Street Walk)
notes: Famous night market street with partial awning cover; good for daytime browsing and snacks

name: Sanxia District Sports Center
notes: Full indoor sports facility including pool and badminton; accessible year-round regardless of weather
